disclaimer: For reference only. Consult your doctor or a travel health clinic before travel.

notes: Tick-borne encephalitis vaccine recommended for forested/rural areas
Tick-borne encephalitis vaccine recommended for forested/rural areas
Tick-borne encephalitis vaccine recommended for forested areas
Tick-borne encephalitis vaccine recommended for Siberia and rural forested areas
Malaria prophylaxis recommended for El Faiyum Governorate only (very limited risk)
Malaria prophylaxis recommended for remote desert areas (very low risk overall)
Meningococcal (quadrivalent) vaccine is required for all Hajj and Umrah pilgrims; required within 10 years before arrival
Japanese Encephalitis vaccine recommended for rural stays longer than 1 month or extensive outdoor activity during transmission season (May–October)
Japanese Encephalitis vaccine recommended for rural areas during transmission season
Japanese Encephalitis vaccine recommended for rural areas and trips longer than 4 weeks
Malaria prophylaxis recommended for rural/jungle areas and border regions with Myanmar and Cambodia (not needed in Bangkok, Chiang Mai city, or major tourist resorts)
Malaria prophylaxis recommended for rural areas, particularly in the Central Highlands and southern provinces (not needed in Hanoi or Ho Chi Minh City)
Malaria prophylaxis recommended for most areas outside Bali, Java, and major cities; Bali has minimal risk but Rabies is present — seek care immediately for any animal bite
Malaria prophylaxis recommended for jungle/rural areas in Borneo (Sabah and Sarawak); not needed in Kuala Lumpur or Penang
Malaria prophylaxis recommended for rural areas on Palawan, Mindanao, and other islands (not needed in Manila or Cebu City)
Malaria prophylaxis recommended for rural areas and border regions (not needed in Phnom Penh or Siem Reap city center)
Malaria prophylaxis recommended outside major cities and in rural/forested areas
Malaria prophylaxis recommended for rural areas and border regions; not needed in Yangon or Mandalay
Malaria prophylaxis recommended for most rural areas; consult your doctor based on specific itinerary
Japanese Encephalitis risk primarily in southern states and during monsoon season
Malaria prophylaxis recommended for the Terai lowlands (not needed above 2000m or in Kathmandu)
Altitude sickness prevention (acetazolamide) recommended for high-altitude trekking above 3000m
Malaria risk is very low in most tourist areas; prophylaxis may be recommended for the North and East provinces
Malaria prophylaxis recommended for most areas below 2500m
Polio booster (IPV) recommended — Pakistan remains a polio-endemic country
Malaria prophylaxis recommended for the Chittagong Hill Tracts
Malaria prophylaxis strongly recommended for all areas below 2500m
Yellow Fever proof of vaccination required if arriving from a Yellow Fever-endemic country; recommended for most travelers visiting areas outside Nairobi city center
Malaria prophylaxis strongly recommended for all mainland areas; Zanzibar also has malaria risk
Yellow Fever proof required if arriving from endemic country; recommended for most travelers
Malaria prophylaxis required for all areas
Mpox (JYNNEOS) vaccine recommended due to ongoing regional outbreak — consult your travel health provider
Malaria prophylaxis required
Mpox (JYNNEOS) vaccine recommended given proximity to ongoing outbreak in the DRC region
Malaria prophylaxis required for all travelers
Mpox (JYNNEOS) vaccine strongly recommended — active outbreak with Clade Ib strain as of 2025–2026
Travel to affected areas requires careful risk assessment; consult a travel health clinic
Malaria prophylaxis required
Mpox (JYNNEOS) vaccine recommended due to ongoing regional outbreak
Malaria prophylaxis recommended for areas below 2500m (not needed in Addis Ababa)
Yellow Fever required if arriving from endemic country; recommended for travelers to some regions
Malaria prophylaxis required for all travelers
Meningococcal vaccine particularly important for travel during dry season to northern regions (meningitis belt)
Malaria prophylaxis required for all travelers

Malaria prophylaxis required for all travelers
Mpox vaccine recommended given proximity to DRC outbreak region
Malaria prophylaxis recommended for Limpopo, Mpumalanga (including Kruger National Park), and KwaZulu-Natal coast below 1000m
Yellow Fever proof required if arriving from endemic country
Malaria prophylaxis recommended for northern and eastern lowveld areas, including Zambezi Valley and Victoria Falls region
Malaria prophylaxis required for all travelers
Yellow Fever recommended for most areas; proof required if arriving from endemic country
Malaria prophylaxis required for all travelers
Yellow Fever proof required if arriving from endemic country
Malaria prophylaxis recommended for rural areas of Chiapas, Oaxaca, Sinaloa, Nayarit, and Tabasco (not needed in major tourist destinations like Cancun, Los Cabos, or Mexico City)
Malaria prophylaxis required for Amazon region (Acre, Amapá, Amazonas, Maranhão, Mato Grosso, Pará, Rondônia, Roraima, Tocantins)
Yellow Fever recommended for most areas; proof of vaccination required for entry to several Brazilian states and may be required at borders
Malaria prophylaxis recommended for Amazon/jungle regions below 2000m (not needed in Lima or Cusco/Machu Picchu area above 2000m)
Yellow Fever required for entry to jungle areas; proof may be required at internal checkpoints
Malaria prophylaxis recommended for rural lowland areas including Pacific coast and Amazon region (not needed in Bogotá or major cities at altitude)
Yellow Fever required for entry to Amazon/jungle areas
Malaria prophylaxis recommended for rural lowland areas below 1500m (not needed in Quito or the Galápagos)
Yellow Fever required for Amazon jungle areas; Galápagos visitors do NOT need Yellow Fever vaccine
Malaria prophylaxis recommended for Amazon lowland areas below 2500m (not needed in La Paz at altitude)
Yellow Fever required for entry to lowland jungle departments; not needed for the Altiplano (La Paz, Potosí, Oruro)
Yellow Fever recommended for travelers to Iguazú Falls and northeastern jungle areas (Misiones, Formosa, Chaco, Corrientes provinces)
No malaria risk in most tourist areas
Very low risk destination; no malaria, no Yellow Fever risk
Malaria prophylaxis recommended for rural areas on the Caribbean coast and Osa Peninsula (not needed in San José or Pacific tourist destinations like Manuel Antonio)
Malaria prophylaxis recommended for Darién region and indigenous Guna Yala (San Blas) islands; not needed in Panama City or other major tourist areas
Malaria prophylaxis recommended for rural areas, particularly western provinces bordering Haiti (not needed in resort areas like Punta Cana or Santo Domingo city)
Malaria prophylaxis required for all travelers
